title: Why Starbucks Is Betting On Employees As Creators
tldr: Starbucks is turning its baristas into content creators, treating employee-generated content as a core brand channel. A concrete case of humanizing a big brand through its people.
angle: Write a playbook on turning employees into your best content channel — governance, incentives, and authenticity trade-offs.

title: Companies Are Judging Comms Pros by Their Own LinkedIn Presence
tldr: Employers increasingly evaluate communications professionals by how they build their own personal brand on LinkedIn. A shift in what comms leadership hiring now values.
angle: Argue that personal thought leadership is now a job requirement, not a bonus, for comms pros.

title: Writing Winning LinkedIn Content, Without AI
tldr: A three-part framework for LinkedIn posts that stop the scroll and drive shares — deliberately human, no AI. Timely counterpoint to AI-generated slop fatigue.
angle: Make the case for human-written content as a differentiator in an AI-saturated feed.

title: How to Write Content That Lands With Decision Makers
tldr: Output is up but senior buyers are less impressed than ever; a practical guide to thought leadership that moves the people who sign contracts. Addresses the content-volume-vs-impact gap.
angle: Reframe B2B content strategy around buyer impact, not dashboard vanity metrics.

title: The secret of Roku City's success as a comms tool
tldr: Roku turned a screensaver into one of its biggest brand assets, full of Easter eggs, memes and cultural references. A rich case study in owned-media and playful brand storytelling.
angle: Break down how an idle-screen 'nothing' became a beloved brand-narrative engine.

title: The 2026 World Cup's First Winner: the Creator Economy
tldr: The World Cup showed creators, not broadcasters, capturing fan attention and brand dollars. Signals a structural shift in how live-event media is consumed.
why_fresh: creator economy reshaping live sports media
angle: Explore how creators are eating broadcasters' lunch around live tentpole events.

title: Mobilizer, connector, spectator, or outsider? New Pew research categorizes how Americans engage civically
tldr: New Pew-Knight research segments Americans by how they engage with news and civic life. Useful audience-psychology framework for understanding media consumption behavior.
angle: Apply Pew's civic-engagement personas to build a media-consumption audience model.

title: Why are people buying so many CDs?
tldr: CD sales rose 16% year-over-year as fans embrace physical media to directly support artists. A telling signal about how people consume — and pay for — content now.
angle: Unpack the physical-media revival as a story about ownership, fandom, and support economies.

title: Netflix says around 300 titles used generative AI
tldr: Netflix confirmed roughly 300 titles used generative AI, mostly in post-production, to cut costs and speed output. A concrete marker of AI's normalization in premium content.
why_fresh: scale of AI in mainstream production disclosed
angle: Discuss what Netflix's AI disclosure means for audience trust and content authenticity.

title: WTF is LLM honeypotting?
tldr: Publishers and ecommerce brands are deploying 'LLM honeypots' to fight aggressive AI crawlers. A fresh tactic in the publisher-vs-AI content-scraping battle.
why_fresh: new defensive tactic against AI crawlers
angle: Explain how publishers are fighting back against AI scraping and what it means for content value.

title: How to Develop AI Guidelines
tldr: Newsroom leaders and policy experts share how to write AI rules that actually work in practice. A practical governance playbook applicable well beyond journalism.
angle: Adapt newsroom AI guidelines into a content-governance template for comms teams.

title: How brands can turn World Cup moments into innovation stories
tldr: Guidance on converting live sports moments into brand narratives, with breakout stars driving fan conversation. Practical campaign angle on real-time storytelling.
angle: Show how brands can build narrative from unscripted live moments rather than pre-planned ads.

title: Anthropic's marketing success holds key lessons for B2B brands
tldr: Anthropic's rise offers a breakdown of B2B marketing done well in the AI era. A timely campaign case study for content-led growth.
why_fresh: fresh B2B case study of an AI-native brand
angle: Extract what Anthropic's positioning teaches about narrative in a crowded AI market.

title: Best Time to Post on Instagram in 2026: New Data from 9.6 Million Posts
tldr: Buffer analyzed 9.6M posts to identify best times, days and formats for Instagram reach. Fresh proprietary data for content-ops planning.
angle: Question whether 'best time to post' data still matters in an algorithm-driven feed.

title: Dove tries Strava to get men buying haircare
tldr: Dove partners with Strava to challenge men to run 5K and upgrade their haircare — an unusual channel-brand pairing. A creative platform-activation case study.
angle: Analyze unexpected platform partnerships as a way to reach hard-to-move audiences.
